channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Senate heads toward confirming Kristi Noem as Trump's homeland security secretary
item.description: The Senate is heading towards a vote on confirming South Dakota Gov. Kristi Noem as homeland security secretary.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Groves, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukrainian winemaker, US vets team up on a mission: showing the best of Ukraine, one glass at a time
item.description: A group of former American military officers and diplomats who served in Kyiv are teaming up with a Ukrainian winery owner to bring a taste of the Black Sea coast to a U.S. audience.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Conservatives of color have lofty expectations for Trump's second term
item.description: President Donald Trump’s pledge to help make Martin Luther King Jr.’s dream a reality is viewed with skepticism by many prominent Black and Hispanic civil rights leaders.
item.dc:creator: Matt Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump proposes 'getting rid of FEMA' while touring disaster areas
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — President Donald Trump surveyed disaster zones in California and North Carolina on Friday and said he was considering “getting rid of” the Federal Emergency Management Agency, offering the latest sign of how he is weighing sweeping changes to the nation's central organization for
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, CHRIS MEGERIAN and MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kristi Noem beats Senate confirmation hurdle, advancing to final vote for DHS role
item.description: Kristi Noem cleared a key procedural hurdle in the Senate on Friday, teeing up her final confirmation vote for early Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Plea to President Trump: Tell Cuba to hand over terrorist killers it is harboring
item.description: Fifty years ago today, the Puerto Rican separatist group, the FALN, bombed Manhattan's historic Fraunces Tavern, but no one has ever been charged in the attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kern County reacts: Trump proposes voter ID mandate for California elections
item.description: Back when 17 News covered that story, we asked viewers if they thought voter ID should be required. More than 700 voted, and 92% said voter ID should be checked at the polls.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Huh, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: State Department freezes new funding for nearly all US aid programs worldwide
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The State Department ordered a sweeping freeze Friday on new funding for almost all U.S. foreign assistance, making exceptions for emergency food programs and military aid to Israel and Egypt. The order threatened a quick halt to many of the billions of dollars in U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Newsom thanks Trump for coming to California to tour fire damage in tarmac face-off
item.description: President Trump and California Gov. Gavin Newsom met on the tarmac of the Los Angeles airport Friday when the president arrived and committed to working with him on wildfire recovery.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pete Hegseth will be confirmed, has 'big shoes to fill': Clark
item.description: "I think it's the president's choice. That's our system," the retired four-star general said ahead of Friday's Senate vote.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Mississippi DA bill would pay bounty hunters a reward to help facilitate deportation of illegal immigrants
item.description: Bounty hunters in Mississippi could be paid by the state to help deport illegal immigrants, under proposed legislation introduced by a lawmaker.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Newsom-Trump war of words still simmering as president arrives in California to survey wildfires
item.description: There's been no let-up in the war of words between President Donald Trump and California Gov. Gavin Newsom as Trump arrives in Los Angeles to survey damage from the recent horrific wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's Cabinet picks are set for Senate hearings. Here's the schedule
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nominations process for President Donald Trump's appointees is picking up pace, with some confirmed by the Senate and hearings scheduled for more of his Cabinet picks. Many nominees have met with senators individually.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump has tossed a lot of Biden policies. But there’s one AI measure he wants to keep
item.description: President Trump says he likes Biden’s idea to open up federal lands for AI data centers. His White House is looking for ways to ensure U.S. dominance in the sector.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: AP Decision Notes: What to expect in Florida's special congressional primaries
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Control of the U.S. House isn’t on the line in Tuesday’s special congressional primaries in Florida, but Republicans are still eager to find replacements for former GOP Reps. Matt Gaetz and Michael Waltz to add some breathing room to their slender majority in the chamber.
item.dc:creator: ROBERT YOON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota justices tell lawmakers to work out control of ‘dysfunctional’ House
item.description: The Minnesota Supreme Court on Friday agreed with DFLers that it takes 68 votes to conduct House business, but the opinion didn’t come with a road map on how the House should proceed from here.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump pulls security for Pompeo, others amid Iran threats
item.description: President Trump pulled security protection for former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo and his top aide, Brian Hook, both of whom have faced threats from Iran amid their hard-line positions toward the Islamic Republic, multiple outlets reported on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: US active duty troops beginning to arrive in Texas, San Diego to support border security
item.description: Active duty military troops will begin arriving in El Paso, Texas, and San Diego on Thursday evening, in what defense officials said is the first batch of the new forces being deployed to secure the southern border.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signs order to declassify files on JFK, RFK and MLK Jr. assassinations
item.description: “Give that to RFK Jr.,” Trump said about the pen he used to sign the order declassifying info about the JFK, RFK and MLK assassinations.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: A federal judge temporarily blocks Trump’s executive order redefining birthright citizenship
item.description: U.S. District Judge John C. Coughenour ruled on Thursday in the case brought by the states of Washington, Arizona, Illinois and Oregon. The states argue that the 14th Amendment and Supreme Court case law have cemented birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump's new Justice Department leadership orders a freeze on civil rights cases
item.description: The new Justice Department leadership has put a freeze on civil rights litigation, and suggested it may reconsider police reform agreements negotiated by the Biden administration. That's according to two memos obtained Wednesday by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump order halting refugee resettlement disrupting Minnesota agencies’ efforts
item.description: Officials with refugee resettlement agencies in Minnesota say Trump’s executive order halting the Refugee Admissions Program is disrupting efforts to bring people facing strife in their home countries to the U.S. and to the state.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: House passes Laken Riley Act, sending it to Trump for first legislative win
item.description: The House on Wednesday passed the Laken Riley Act, sending the immigration-related bill to President Trump’s desk in what is poised to be his first legislative victory since returning to the White House this week. The chamber cleared the bill in a 263-156 vote.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term, though still below the majority support that most other presidents have enjoyed at the start of their administrations.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: Plan to reform 300 Nevada boards, commissions emerges
item.description: After Gov. Joe Lombardo took aim in his State of the State address at an ever-expanding number of boards and commissions that operate in Nevada with minimal oversight, a plan to reform the system is emerging.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What exactly is an executive order? Examining Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: Donald Trump returned to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump declares energy emergency
item.description: President Trump has declared an energy emergency, which his team has said would unlock additional powers to jumpstart production.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Trump sworn in as 47th president, celebrates with supporters
item.description: On a frigid holiday Monday in Washington, D.C., Donald Trump took the oath of office for a second time to become the 47th president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, PIX 11

item.title: Biden pardons Fauci, Jan. 6 committee ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: (The Hill) -- President Joe Biden granted pardons to several prominent public servants Monday who have faced attacks from President-elect Donald Trump in one of his final acts in office. Biden issued pardons for Dr.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5
